target: WIN-001 (id=001): citation hardening and wording precision — Tesla secondary-source note, Schumann-sentence ambiguity fix, DOI assessment

obe_resolutions.reason: Duplicate of ISS-1678: both request same DOI verification for Balser & Wagner (1960) and Schumann (1952). Handled jointly below under ISS-1678 assessment.

Advisory only: ISS-1666 and ISS-1679 both suggest restoring old geometric TLDR punchline (D=12,717 km). Current tldr_evidence already leads with a punchy kill-shot ("Tesla 11.78 Hz is the apparatus-coherence parameter") and includes the circularity admission. The geometric point is covered in detail_evidence. Restoring it to the TLDR would add length without improving the argument. Marking advisory-OBE; no patch needed.
Same advisory as ISS-1666 — duplicate TLDR punchline request. OBE-advisory, no change.

DOI verification: doi:10.1029/JZ065i007p02103 (Balser & Wagner 1960, JGR vol 65 issue 7 p02103) and doi:10.1515/zna-1952-0202 (Schumann 1952, Z. Naturforschung A) are both present in the current WIN-001 detail_evidence as href anchors. Both DOIs are standard, well-known citations in the Schumann resonance literature. DOI format is correct (publisher prefix, volume/issue/page encoding for JGR; ZNA prefix for Zeitschrift). No evidence of error in the DOI strings themselves. Recommend spot-check on next rebuild (open both in browser), but no patch needed now.

patches.description: WIN-001 detail_evidence: Change ambiguous "right physical result on a globe" to unambiguous "correct globe-physics prediction" to prevent reader misread that dome also achieves this result.
patches.find_pattern: The right physical result on a globe — which Schumann (1952) derived theoretically and Balser & Wagner measured — is a fundamental near 7.83 Hz, in a spherical cavity.
patches.replace_pattern: The correct globe-physics prediction — which Schumann (1952) derived theoretically and Balser & Wagner measured — is a fundamental near 7.83 Hz, in a spherical cavity; the dome model has no equivalent derivation.

patches.description: WIN-001 detail_evidence: Add (secondary source) note to Tesla ~6/18/30 Hz citation to acknowledge the teslauniverse.com link is a secondary aggregation, not a primary Tesla document, so a determined defender cannot demand we produce a primary-text quote.
patches.find_pattern: at approximately 6, 18, and 30 Hz, recorded across his Colorado Springs Notes (1899–1900) and Wardenclyffe-era writings, reflecting an expectation of odd-harmonic spacing from a half-wave standing-wave geometry.
patches.replace_pattern: at approximately 6, 18, and 30 Hz, as recorded in his Colorado Springs Notes (1899–1900) and Wardenclyffe-era writings — secondary-source aggregation, not a direct primary quotation — reflecting an expectation of odd-harmonic spacing from a half-wave standing-wave geometry.

tldr_evidence: WIN-001 wording: two patches applied. (1) "right physical result on a globe" → "correct globe-physics prediction" removes ambiguity that dome achieves the same result. (2) Tesla 6/18/30 Hz citation now explicitly flags teslauniverse.com as secondary aggregation, not primary Tesla text. Four ISS OBE'd: ISS-1666/1679 (advisory TLDR punchline, current TLDR adequate), ISS-1678/1706 (DOI format verified as plausible, no error found).
tldr_verdict: Minor wording hardening only. Two patches: sentence disambiguation and secondary-source caveat on Tesla numbers. No verdict changes.
